# GitHub Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview
This is a Sorting Algorithms Visualizer built with plain HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript.
It is a beginner-friendly lab project that follows a simple MVC (Model-View-Controller) structure.

## Technology Rules
- Use only HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript
- Do NOT use React, Angular, Vue, jQuery, Bootstrap, Tailwind, or TypeScript
- Do NOT import any external libraries or CDNs

## Architecture
This project uses a simple MVC pattern split into:
- `js/model.js`      → Data and sorting algorithm logic
- `js/view.js`       → DOM rendering and UI updates
- `js/controller.js` → Event handling, connects Model and View
- `js/app.js`        → Entry point that starts the application

## Coding Style
- Use simple functions, not classes
- Prefer `let` for variables that change, `const` for fixed values
- Keep functions small and focused on one job
- Add friendly comments explaining what each function does
- Avoid complex patterns or over-engineering

## CSS Style
- Use simple flexbox-based layout
- No gradients, no heavy animations
- Soft colors, clean spacing, rounded buttons
- Keep it student-friendly and easy to read

## Comments
- Write comments in a friendly, educational tone
- Explain the "why" and "what" of important steps
- Keep comments short and clear
- Comments should help a student understand the code easily
